engine.goal: Fix the send-money insufficient-balance top-up UX so it asks for top-up method first, supports internal UNIGOX username funding, and keeps external deposits token -> chain -> single-address.

inspect_current_topup_flow.root_cause: The insufficient-balance branch jumped straight from a blocked balance check to a generic fund-wallet message. It never forced a top-up method choice first, so users who wanted another UNIGOX user to fund them were pushed into the external deposit token/chain flow by default.

design_topup_fix.design_notes: Insert an explicit top-up-method step after insufficient balance, add internal-username vs external-deposit subflows, keep recheck/change-amount behavior intact, and reuse the existing frontend-filtered external deposit token -> chain -> single-address flow only when the user actually chooses external deposit.
design_topup_fix.flow_sequence: insufficient balance -> ask top-up method -> internal UNIGOX username instructions OR external deposit token -> chain -> single address -> recheck balance -> return to confirmation when funded

validate_topup_fix.test_results: PASS (23/23 tests). Added regressions for insufficient-balance top-up method prompting, internal UNIGOX username funding, and external token -> chain -> single-address deposit sequencing.

commit_topup_fix.commit_sha: captured in git history / task summary
